summary: Second-life EV battery storage systems for commercial sites.
editor_note: The better climate bet is not another new battery chemistry; it is making retired EV packs insurable infrastructure. Moment Energy leads with Luna BESS, UL 1974 and UL 9540A certifications, supply ties to Mercedes-Benz and Nissan, and an Austin factory plan. Unlike generic grid-storage pitches, the product choice is narrow: own the battery management layer so old packs can safely serve data centers and industrial sites.
investors: Evok Innovations, Liberty Mutual Investments, W23 Global Fund, Acario

product_evidence.claim: Official product positioning describes Moment Energy: Second-life EV battery storage systems for commercial sites.
Moment Energy leads with Luna BESS, UL 1974 and UL 9540A certifications, supply ties to Mercedes-Benz and Nissan, and an Austin factory plan.

market_context.primary_user: Climate, infrastructure, or operations teams evaluating measurable sustainability workflows.
market_context.why_now: Recent source-backed funding and public product evidence make battery storage, climate, energy infrastructure worth tracking.

risks.claim: Continued tracking should verify whether the public product evidence turns into durable customer adoption beyond the currently visible surface.
